does: This paper introduces HoloFair, a comprehensive benchmark framework for evaluating multidimensional demographic biases in text-to-image models, along with Fair-GRPO, a reinforcement learning debiasing method. The framework leverages a large-scale fairness-oriented dataset and a Spatial-Frequency attribute classifier to compute the Multi-attribute, Group-wise Bias Index (MGBI), which captures both intrinsic diversity and conditional biases across multiple demographic dimensions simultaneously. Beyond evaluation, the authors develop Fair-GRPO, which uses reinforcement learning with a multi-objective reward function to alter the generative model's distribution and reduce biases. Experiments on SD3.5-Medium demonstrate that Fair-GRPO substantially improves multidimensional fairness metrics while preserving image quality, and the authors examine potential reward hacking issues that arise during the debiasing process.
connects: This work builds on a growing body of research addressing bias and fairness in generative models, extending prior single-dimensional bias evaluation methods toward holistic multidimensional assessment. It responds to limitations in existing T2I fairness work by developing deeper semantic-level bias detection that goes beyond surface-level demographic representation. The paper connects to reinforcement learning approaches for model alignment and debiasing, drawing on techniques like GRPO that have been applied to language models. It also engages with broader work on fairness metrics design, building on evaluation frameworks from computer vision and NLP that assess group-level disparities and intrinsic diversity. The use of spatial-frequency analysis for attribute classification reflects advances in fine-grained visual understanding relevant to bias detection.
enables: This work enables future research into more sophisticated multidimensional fairness evaluation across generative models, providing both methodological tools and empirical baselines for the community. The HoloFair framework and MGBI metric can be adapted to other generative modalities and models, establishing standards for fairness benchmarking. Fair-GRPO demonstrates the viability of RL-based debiasing at scale and opens directions for developing more nuanced reward functions that balance fairness objectives with other criteria like image quality and semantic fidelity. The investigation of reward hacking provides important cautionary insights for future RL-based alignment work. These contributions set the foundation for deploying fairer T2I models in practice and inform broader efforts to build generative systems that serve diverse user populations equitably.
